nodes.summary: Added an admin-only account filter to the routes table so admins can filter routes by owner name, while keeping the existing Latest/A-Z sort. The selected owner filter persists in localStorage per signed-in admin and resets safely when invalid or cleared. Route workspace now passes current user identity into the table, and the filter logic was isolated into a small preferences helper.

affectedFiles.reason: Added admin-only owner filtering, persisted selection wiring, and empty-state clearing behavior in the routes table UI.
affectedFiles.notes: Also now accepts current user identity props from the workspace.

affectedFiles.reason: Passed current user identity to the route table so filter persistence can be scoped per signed-in user and account labels can be rendered.
affectedFiles.notes: No backend changes were required.

affectedFiles.reason: Centralized localStorage keying and read/write helpers for the persisted account filter preference.
affectedFiles.notes: Keeps storage access out of the main table component.

nodes.summary: Converted the admin users timestamp from last-login to last-active semantics. The backend now stores and refreshes lastActiveAt whenever an authenticated request resolves a user session, while preserving lastLoginAt as a compatibility fallback for older records. The frontend admin users table and mobile user cards now show Last active instead of Last login.

affectedFiles.reason: Added lastActiveAt persistence, compatibility fallback serialization, and the TouchLastActive storage method used by authenticated access.
affectedFiles.notes: Also kept lastLoginAt as a deprecated alias in the public JSON contract.

affectedFiles.reason: Refreshed the active timestamp on successful login, password-based session renewal, and every authenticated request that passes requireUser.
affectedFiles.notes: The handler now writes back the current active time into the in-memory user before returning auth responses.

affectedFiles.reason: Renamed the managed user timestamp contract to lastActiveAt so the admin UI matches the new backend field.
affectedFiles.notes: The frontend type no longer depends on lastLoginAt.

affectedFiles.reason: Updated the admin users table and mobile cards to label and render Last active using the new timestamp field.
affectedFiles.notes: Kept the existing visual layout and added a neutral empty-state message for users with no activity yet.

nodes.summary: Adjusted the router maintenance page so the entire 503 screen is centered in the viewport by moving centering behavior to the outer document/body layer and keeping the shell content unchanged. Verified the router package still builds and tests cleanly.

affectedFiles.reason: Updated the maintenance page template CSS so the full viewport page centers correctly.
affectedFiles.notes: Adjusted html/body sizing and centering, and moved the padding/min-height behavior to the outer layer.

nodes.summary: Updated the router maintenance page heading so 'temporarily unavailable.' stays on one unbroken line while preserving the viewport-centering fixes and avoiding horizontal overflow by reducing the responsive heading scale.

affectedFiles.reason: Adjusted the maintenance page template heading wrapping and responsive font sizing.
affectedFiles.notes: Restored the nowrap span around 'temporarily unavailable.' and changed the h1 clamp to use 8vw.

nodes.summary: Restructured the router maintenance page centering after repeated right-alignment complaints. The template now uses a dedicated fixed .viewport wrapper with inset 0 and grid place-items center, while main is a bounded centered column and heading lines center independently. This keeps 'is temporarily unavailable.' on one line without letting it shift the whole screen right.

affectedFiles.reason: Restructured maintenance page HTML and CSS to fix persistent right-alignment/centering issues.
affectedFiles.notes: Added .viewport wrapper, changed body overflow/min-height behavior, bounded main, and centered heading lines independently.

nodes.summary: Investigated a Routes admin-page name mismatch where admin saw WAH4H/WAH4P drift. Root cause was a denormalized route.ownerName snapshot in MongoDB; the backend Routes list previously returned the stored snapshot directly. I changed the registry list path to re-resolve current owner names from the users collection on read, with stored ownerName as fallback. Added a unit test for owner-name fallback and documented the behavior in the backend README. Targeted backend tests for registry and api passed; full `go test ./...` still has a separate pre-existing registry test failure in destination tests.

affectedFiles.reason: Added user-based owner-name resolution in the route list path and preserved snapshot fallback logic.
affectedFiles.notes: Introduced a users collection reference, owner-name lookup helper, and display-name override conversion.

affectedFiles.reason: Added coverage for the owner-name override/fallback behavior used by route listing.
affectedFiles.notes: Pure unit test for the public conversion helper.

affectedFiles.reason: Documented that route list responses resolve current owner names from the users collection.
affectedFiles.notes: Clarifies denormalized snapshot behavior for future maintainers.

nodes.summary: Unified the settings sidebar and main content into a single connected shell in the RouteGate frontend. The settings workspace now uses one shared card container with an internal divider and full-width overflow clipping so the panels visually read as one integrated layout rather than separate cards.

affectedFiles.reason: Adjusted the settings shell markup so the sidebar and content area share one card container instead of separate cards.
affectedFiles.notes: Removed per-panel card styling and kept the section content intact.

affectedFiles.reason: Updated the settings workspace styles to remove the visual gap between panels and add an internal divider for the unified shell.
affectedFiles.notes: Made the sidebar/content relationship feel connected while preserving responsiveness.

nodes.summary: Refined the RouteGate settings sidebar so its navigation buttons are flush, full-width rows inside the panel rather than card-like pills. The sidebar now uses a full-bleed list with no internal gaps, and the active state is an inset accent instead of a separate bordered chip.

affectedFiles.reason: Reworked the settings sidebar layout so buttons are flush, full-width rows with no extra gaps and no card styling.
affectedFiles.notes: Added full-bleed nav rows, removed rounded button cards, and switched the active treatment to an inset accent.

affectedFiles.reason: Verified the shell markup that wraps the sidebar and main content so the CSS change could stay localized.
affectedFiles.notes: No additional markup change was needed in this pass.

nodes.summary: Refined the RouteGate settings shell to keep the panel height stable and the sidebar visually clean. The settings workspace now uses a fixed-height shell with scrollable content, a white sidebar with a full-height right border, and row-separated navigation items with bottom borders and no left active marker.

affectedFiles.reason: Updated the settings shell layout, sidebar border treatment, active state styling, and fixed-height behavior.
affectedFiles.notes: The workspace is fixed-height and the content area scrolls internally.

affectedFiles.reason: Confirmed the shell composition still cleanly separates the sidebar from the active settings panel.
affectedFiles.notes: No markup change was required for this pass.
